root_cause.text: The `cine_read_header` function parses image offsets based on an attacker-controlled "duration" field without verifying if the end-of-file has been reached, leading to excessive resource consumption.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces an `avio_feof(pb)` check inside the `for (i = 0; i < st->duration; i++)` loop in `libavformat/cinedec.c` to abort if the file ends before all claimed offsets are read.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `cine_read_header` function parses image offsets based on an attacker-controlled "duration" field without verifying if the end-of-file has been reached, leading to excessive resource consumption.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces an `avio_feof(pb)` check inside the `for (i = 0; i < st->duration; i++)` loop in `libavformat/cinedec.c` to abort if the file ends before all claimed offsets are read.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add an EOF guard during image offset parsing to prevent invalid reads.
intent_record.what: Added an EOF check using avio_feof inside the image offsets parsing loop.
intent_record.how: Prevents reading beyond the end of the file if the stated duration exceeds the available data.

grouping_result.intent: Add an EOF guard during image offset parsing to prevent invalid reads.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding an EOF check during image offset parsing, which prevents the excessive resource consumption caused by the attacker-controlled duration field.

candidates.rationale: This line performs file reads and allocates index entries inside a loop controlled by the attacker-controlled 'duration' without checking for end-of-file.
This loop uses the attacker-controlled 'duration' field to parse image offsets without verifying if the end-of-file has been reached.

vuln_statements.explanation: This loop iterates over the attacker-controlled 'st->duration' without any EOF checks, leading to excessive resource consumption.
This statement reads from the file without verifying if EOF has been reached and allocates memory for index entries, driven by the unbounded loop.

bic_trace_step.reason: The function `cine_read_header` contains a loop `for (i = 0; i < st->duration; i++)` that reads image offsets using `avio_rl64(pb)` based on the attacker-controlled `st->duration`. There are no checks to verify if the end-of-file has been reached or if memory limits are exceeded, which causes excessive resource consumption.

bic_trace_step.reason: The `cine_read_header` function loops `for (i = 0; i < st->duration; i++) av_add_index_entry(st, avio_rl64(pb), i, 0, 0, AVINDEX_KEYFRAME);` based purely on the attacker-controlled `st->duration` value, without verifying if the end-of-file has been reached.
